checks.name: schema_completeness
checks.detail: All required payload fields present: structured_prompt, suggested_tools, suggested_triggers, suggested_connectors, use_case_flows, full_prompt_markdown, summary, design_highlights

checks.name: flow_integrity
checks.detail: 2 flows (flow_batch_enrichment: 15 nodes/16 edges, flow_weekly_catalog_health: 12 nodes connected). All start/end nodes present, edges valid.

checks.name: trigger_validity
checks.detail: 2 schedule triggers with valid cron expressions (0 6 * * * daily, 0 9 * * 1 weekly)

checks.name: adoption_question_fitness
checks.detail: 5 adoption_questions added covering spreadsheet_id, sheet_name, writing_tone, batch_size, slack_channel

checks.name: safety_scan
checks.detail: 0 critical findings. No credential exposure, no destructive operations without checks.

dimensions.name: Prompt Completeness
dimensions.rationale: Identity is 110+ words with clear persona. Instructions are 2000+ words with 7 numbered sections. Tool guidance covers Google Sheets and Slack APIs with specific endpoints and JSON bodies. Examples include 3 detailed scenarios (electronics, fashion, batch summary). Error handling is comprehensive with per-service strategies.

dimensions.name: Tool-Prompt Alignment
dimensions.rationale: All tools (http_request, file_read, file_write) are referenced in prompt with concrete API calls. No phantom tools. Slight gap: prompt mentions 'AI content generation' but no explicit LLM tool reference — acceptable since the persona IS the AI.

dimensions.name: Trigger Coherence
dimensions.rationale: Daily 6 AM batch enrichment and Monday 9 AM weekly report are both described in detail in the prompt instructions and match the trigger configs exactly.

dimensions.name: Connector Coverage
dimensions.rationale: Service flow [Google Sheets, Slack] fully backed by google_sheets and slack connectors with complete credential fields and setup instructions.

dimensions.name: Memory Design
dimensions.rationale: Enrichment queue (data/enrichment_queue.json) tracks SKU-level state with hashes for change detection. Weekly reports archived. Agent memory for enrichment patterns mentioned. Good scope for the use case.

dimensions.name: Error Handling
dimensions.rationale: Covers Google Sheets 401/403/404/429/400/500, content generation failures (insufficient data, too short, too long, duplicates), Slack failures, data integrity (queue corruption, sheet structure changes, concurrent edits), and edge cases (non-English, large catalogs, first-run). Excellent coverage.

dimensions.name: Use Case Fidelity
dimensions.rationale: Both use_case_flows (batch enrichment and weekly health report) are fully covered in the prompt instructions with matching step-by-step procedures.

dimensions.name: Value Clarity
dimensions.rationale: Clear business outcome: transforms sparse product data into SEO-optimized catalog content at scale. Replaces content team manual work. Quantified batch sizes and progress tracking.

dimensions.name: Execution Feasibility
dimensions.rationale: Google Sheets API and Slack API are standard and well-documented. OAuth2 for Google Sheets adds adoption complexity. All trigger configs are actionable. The AI content generation is the persona's native capability.

dimensions.name: Differentiation
dimensions.rationale: Adds significant reasoning: tone selection, quality checks, duplicate detection, category suggestion, re-enrichment detection. Far beyond what a simple cron+API workflow could do.

dimensions.name: Variable Necessity
dimensions.rationale: All 5 adoption questions are genuinely needed: spreadsheet_id and sheet_name are required for operation, tone and batch_size customize behavior, slack_channel routes notifications.

dimensions.name: Default Quality
dimensions.rationale: Good defaults for sheet_name (Sheet1), writing_tone (Professional), batch_size (25), slack_channel (#ecommerce-catalog). Missing defaults for spreadsheet_id (required, no sensible default). User must provide 1-2 values to get started.

issues: No adoption_questions existed — 5 added covering all user-specific config values
Google Sheets OAuth2 connector may not have a builtin definition, adding adoption friction
Spreadsheet ID has no default (unavoidable — user-specific)

template: Added 5 adoption_questions: spreadsheet_id, sheet_name, writing_tone, batch_size, slack_channel
Questions include select types with options for tone and batch_size for constrained choices
